title: Streaming JSON, CSV, Accessible HTML, and Remediation Workflows · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers streaming json, csv, accessible html, and remediation workflows into a changed deterministic accessible report suite. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of deterministic accessible report suite with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Version record envelopes, ordering, missingness, error rows, provenance, encoding, newline, and digest manifests for streaming JSON.
Define headers, ordering, delimiter, quoting, newline, Unicode, nulls, formula defense, row limits, and round-trip tests with encoding/csv.
Sort every externally visible map-derived set by stable typed keys and separate semantic order from serialization order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

lab case — A lab Go team handles case 5791 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove streaming JSON, CSV dialect, deterministic order, accessible HTML, and remediation lineage; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Remediation Lineage
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-remediation-lineage-si8f8" that uses case 7845 to prove streaming JSON, CSV dialect, deterministic order, accessible HTML, and remediation lineage. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Remediation Lineage.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Remediation Lineage into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Remediation Lineage, keep Remediation Lineage connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Remediation Lineage failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Removing a finding row is enough remediation history.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Link finding evidence, owner, severity rationale, status, exception expiry, correction, retest, changed page, and stakeholder outcome.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Remediation Lineage condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
